STRATEGIES.title: Don't do all of your reading
STRATEGIES.tagline: Read for the argument. Not for completion.
moves: Read the intro and conclusion slow. Skim the rest, ticking the sentences that carry the argument.
Lecture notes are your source of truth. The textbook is backup.
Own your Sundays. A sharp morning sets the whole week.
Drop a bad course in week one. Bad courses are kryptonite.
STRATEGIES.quote: If you take control of your Sunday, you take control of your week.

STRATEGIES.title: Make your bed
moves: Make the bed. Clear the floor. Trash out. Every day, on autopilot.
Roommate's a mess? Keep your half spotless anyway.
Apply for scholarships every year. Most go unclaimed because nobody bothers.
Build a study system with checklists. Then the material never feels like a wall.
STRATEGIES.quote: A clean room creates a focused mind; a messy room, a distracted one.

STRATEGIES.title: Read a newspaper every day
STRATEGIES.tagline: Give your brain a daily workout.
moves: Read a serious paper every day. It sharpens you and widens your view.
Get good at one thing. Real skill builds quiet confidence.
Don't rent your self-worth from other people's approval. Own it.
Plan in time blocks, not to-do lists. College days are too jagged for lists.
STRATEGIES.quote: Reading a newspaper is a daily workout for the brain.

STRATEGIES.title: Sign up for something your first week
STRATEGIES.tagline: Get in before the awkwardness hardens.
moves: Join a club, team, or paper in week one. Beat the isolation.
Think bigger. Design one Grand Project worth years of your time.
Take classes outside your major. Build real range.
Pick one class a semester and truly excel in it.
STRATEGIES.quote: Your first week is the best time to get involved.

STRATEGIES.title: Jump into research as soon as possible
STRATEGIES.tagline: Research is where the action is.
moves: Email a professor about one specific thing. Enthusiasm beats credentials.
Start humble. Be useful, nail the small tasks, then earn more.
Research isn't only for scientists. Every field has open questions.
Work in 50-minute sprints. Take the ten.
STRATEGIES.quote: Research is where all the action is.

STRATEGIES.title: Decorate your room
STRATEGIES.tagline: Build a room that sounds like you.
moves: Skip the generic posters. Hang what you actually love.
Good light. A chair you'd sit in. Comfort changes everything.
Start exam prep two weeks out, in small daily chunks. Never the all-nighter.
Treat writing as a core skill. Practice it on purpose.
STRATEGIES.quote: Make a space that's both comfortable and inspiring.

STRATEGIES.title: Take hard courses early on
STRATEGIES.tagline: Don't hide in the minor leagues.
moves: Cap intro courses at half your schedule. The advanced ones teach more.
Study in the library. The dorm is where focus goes to die.
Learn it solo first. Lean on the study group only after.
Join an honors program. Smaller rooms, sharper people.
STRATEGIES.quote: Why spend college in the minor leagues?

STRATEGIES.title: Attend guest lectures
STRATEGIES.tagline: Go to get set on fire.
moves: Hit two guest lectures a month. They relight your curiosity.
Make exercise non-negotiable. Same time, decided once.
Call your home friends monthly. Voice, not text.
Add a major or minor. It rarely adds course load.
STRATEGIES.quote: You go to ignite some passion within yourself.

STRATEGIES.title: Don't get a normal job
STRATEGIES.tagline: Take work that pays you twice.
moves: Pick an academic or quiet desk job you can study during.
Write short papers over three days. Draft, edit, polish. Never in one sitting.
Learn your real sleep need. Then hold the schedule.
The hour before an exam? Relax, don't cram.
STRATEGIES.quote: Don't spend your hours on a job that drains your studies.

STRATEGIES.title: Don't binge drink
STRATEGIES.tagline: Protect the edge you came for.
moves: Drink if you want. Just don't binge.
Stop comparing grades. They're not measuring the same thing.
Set big goals. Then go find the people already there.
Listen more than you talk. Synthesize before you speak.
STRATEGIES.quote: You can drink and still win. You just can't binge and win.

STRATEGIES.title: Laugh every day
STRATEGIES.tagline: Stress is the enemy. Laughter is the cure.
moves: Get one real laugh every day. Friends, comedy, whatever lands.
Buy supplies that kill friction. Spend the focus on thinking, not hunting.
Keep a work-progress journal. Planned versus done, honestly.
Schedule fun on purpose. A packed term erases it otherwise.
STRATEGIES.quote: Life is short, and the benefits of laughter are enormous.

STRATEGIES.title: Get involved with your major department
STRATEGIES.tagline: Your department is a network in disguise.
moves: Treat the department like a company you want to be known inside.
Show up to one department event a month. Bring a real question.
Chase understanding, not GPA. Bombed an exam? Learn from it and move.
Lecture attendance is non-negotiable.
STRATEGIES.quote: Don't avoid them. Get involved with your major department.

STRATEGIES.title: Volunteer quietly
moves: Volunteer without seeking credit. The quiet of it builds real self-worth.
Write with style. Pull the reader in, don't just inform them.
Engage with real issues. It sharpens your sense of purpose.
Plan summer early. For growth, not just rest.
STRATEGIES.quote: Volunteer quietly. It strengthens your sense of self-worth.

STRATEGIES.title: Don't take breaks between classes
moves: Use the gaps for small tasks or errands. Don't drift back to the dorm to rest.
Try antinetworking. Real curiosity about people, no hidden agenda.
Write one op-ed a semester for the student paper.
Get organized. Then the work flows instead of stalling.

STRATEGIES.title: Study with the quiz-and-recall method
moves: Review once, silent, noting the questions the material should answer.
Then quiz yourself out loud, in full sentences, until you ace it cold.
Add emotion and movement. Pace, stand, react. It locks memory in.
Keep your inbox and files clean. Less background stress.
STRATEGIES.quote: Your goal is to ace the quiz without ever peeking.

STRATEGIES.title: Don't have no regrets
moves: Chase experiences over trophies. That's where the fulfillment lives.
Treat a failure as gratitude for the swing, not regret for the miss.
Be ambitious for your own sake. Not for the applause.
Find the adventure in the work itself.
